ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'sandwich' class (FOODON_00002473). No specific multigrain ham and cheese sandwich entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers sandwiches as a category. No multigrain-specific or ham-and-cheese-specific code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 is the generic sandwich food concept. No multigrain ham and cheese variant exists; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One multigrain ham and cheese sandwich weighing approximately 150 g, consisting of two slices of multigrain bread with one to two slices of ham and one slice of processed cheese, typically toasted.

nutrient_source.title: Ham and Cheese Sandwich — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact multigrain ham and cheese sandwich entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for multigrain bread (FDC ID 325871), cooked ham (FDC ID 174608), and processed American cheese (FDC ID 173414), combined in proportions consistent with a standard 150 g sandwich (approximately 60 g multigrain bread, 40 g ham, 20 g processed cheese, 10 g butter/margarine, 20 g lettuce/condiments). Per-100 g values derived by weighted average of component contributions.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a multigrain ham and cheese sandwich. GI of 56 is estimated from the GI of multigrain bread (GI ~43–65, mean ~54 per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international tables) as the dominant glycemic contributor. Protein and fat from ham and cheese slow gastric emptying, moderating the effective GI slightly upward from plain multigrain bread toward the medium range. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 39.75) / 100 ≈ 22.3, where carbohydrate per 150 g serving = 26.5 × 1.5 = 39.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from processed ham and cheese
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 680 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1020 mg per 150 g serving), this sandwich contributes over 40% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals; those managing blood pressure should limit portion size, request reduced-sodium ham, or avoid added condiments such as soy sauce or ketchup.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index and moderate glycemic load from multigrain bread
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 56 (medium) and a glycemic load of approximately 22 per serving, this sandwich produces a moderate postprandial glucose response; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size, pair with non-starchy vegetables, and prefer versions with higher-fibre multigrain bread to further attenuate the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from processed ham and cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of approximately 4.1 g saturated fat and 680 mg sodium per 100 g from processed ham and cheese raises cardiovascular concern; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption frequency, choose lower-fat cheese alternatives, and select lean ham to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Multigrain Ham and Cheese Sandwich is a toasted or assembled sandwich served in Singaporean café chains such as Toast Box, consisting of two slices of multigrain bread filled with one or two slices of processed ham and a slice of processed cheese, often with butter or margarine. Multigrain bread provides dietary fibre (approximately 2.8 g per 100 g) and a moderate glycemic index compared to white bread, making it a relatively more nutritious bread choice. The ham contributes protein (approximately 12.8 g per 100 g) and a significant sodium load, while the processed cheese adds calcium, saturated fat, and additional sodium. The sandwich is typically toasted on a sandwich press or grill, giving it a warm, slightly crispy exterior with a soft, melted interior. In the Singaporean café context, it is commonly served as a breakfast or light meal item, often accompanied by a hot beverage such as kopi (local coffee) or teh (local tea). Nutritionally, the sandwich is a moderate-energy food at approximately 248 kcal per 100 g, with a balanced macronutrient profile, but its high sodium content (approximately 680 mg per 100 g) is a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The medium GI and moderate glycemic load require portion awareness for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The multigrain bread base provides B vitamins, iron, and dietary fibre that support general metabolic health.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for a multigrain ham and cheese sandwich was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for multigrain bread, cooked ham, and processed cheese in proportions consistent with a standard 150 g sandwich. GI is estimated from the multigrain bread GI anchor in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international tables. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and based on approved sources, but no direct measurement of this specific product exists.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for this estimation. The input record listed food_group as 'Dairy and eggs', which has been corrected to 'Mixed' as this is a composite sandwich product containing bread, meat, and dairy. The input description ('Each set comes with 1 large beverage and 1 cake') refers to a meal set context at Toast Box and is not a nutritional description of the sandwich itself; it has been replaced with a factual food description. The field dataed (Toast Box - Funan) is a Singaporean café chain known for local toast and coffee items. Price range SGD 5.80–12.30 likely reflects the set meal price including beverage and cake, not the sandwich alone. Nutrient values reflect the sandwich component only, not the set meal. GI of 56 is an analogous estimate based on multigrain bread as the primary glycemic contributor; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (56 × 39.75) / 100 ≈ 22.3 g, where carbohydrate per 150 g serving = 39.75 g.
